source.title: PDD Holdings Insider Ownership and SBC Analysis

extractions.claim: Colin Huang remains PDD's largest shareholder at ~20-26.5% but has progressively reduced his stake through charitable donations, signaling long-term commitment over personal enrichment
extractions.evidence: Colin Huang held 43.3% pre-IPO, reduced to 29.4% by June 2020 through donations (2.37% to Starry Night charitable trust, 7.74% to Pinduoduo Partnership). As of March 2024, stake was ~26.5%. By 2025-2026 estimates range 20-25%. He donated 12.8% of shares worth >$10B to charitable/institutional entities. He extended share lockup by 3 years and gave up 1:10 super voting rights, entrusting voting to the board.

verification.verifier_notes: Ownership figures confirmed across Wikipedia, Yahoo Finance, DCFmodeling, and multiple analyst reports. Exact current stake uncertain due to foreign private issuer disclosure rules (no Form 4 required).

extractions.claim: PDD's SBC as % of revenue is declining — from ~2.9% in 2023 to ~2.5% in 2024 — indicating disciplined equity dilution despite absolute SBC rising

verification.verifier_notes: SBC figures from MacroTrends. Revenue from PDD earnings releases. Ratio calculation verified.

extractions.claim: PDD's SBC burden is far lower than global e-commerce peers on a revenue-percentage basis
extractions.evidence: PDD SBC/Revenue ~2.5% in 2024. For context, Chinese tech peers like Alibaba and JD typically run 3-5% SBC/revenue, while US companies like Amazon run higher. Shopify and Sea Limited have historically had much higher SBC ratios (5-15% of revenue).

verification.verifier_notes: PDD figure is confirmed. Peer comparisons are approximate based on general industry knowledge. Exact peer SBC/revenue ratios would need individual verification.

extractions.claim: Insider ownership at PDD is concentrated in founder Colin Huang (~20-26%) and Tencent (~14%), with institutional ownership at ~28%
extractions.evidence: Colin Huang: ~20-26.5% (largest individual). Tencent Holdings: ~14% (second largest). Institutional investors: ~28.4% (including Baillie Gifford ~2.4%, BlackRock, Vanguard, Mirae Asset). General public: ~25%. Reported insider ownership at 4.53% (excluding Huang's beneficial ownership through BVI entities).

verification.verifier_notes: Ownership breakdown confirmed across multiple sources. The 4.53% insider figure likely excludes Huang's shares held through BVI entities.
